description: submit an async deep-research job to a reachable AI-Q backend, poll, and print the final report JSON
rules: requires a reachable AI-Q Blueprint backend; does not deploy one
user query text is transmitted to AIQ_SERVER_URL — confirm the endpoint is trusted before sending sensitive data
non-local AIQ_SERVER_URL values must use https and be explicitly trusted by the user
does not collect or manage API keys / bearer tokens / cookies; credentials live in the backend environment
stops on failed jobs and surfaces the returned error; never retries a failed job automatically
report + audit line are written under record_store; citations and source URLs are kept intact
usage: Set AIQ_QUERY (the research question). Optionally set AIQ_AGENT_TYPE (default shallow_researcher) and AIQ_SERVER_URL (default http://localhost:8000). Output: report.json.
limitation: Requires a running AI-Q backend; it does not deploy one. The public helper does not manage authentication tokens or cookies. Remote AIQ_SERVER_URL endpoints may log prompts, responses, and metadata. If the backend returns HTTP 500 or lacks async agents, the failure is reported rather than fabricating a research answer. Designed for AI-Q Blueprint 2.1.0 (major version must match).

vars.AIQ_QUERY: Compare local AIQ deep research with a standard web search workflow
